topic.what: Clinical review of bile reflux gastropathy covering pathophysiology, diagnosis, and management including post-surgical bile reflux and ursodeoxycholic acid therapy
topic.how: Expert-opinion narrative review synthesising evidence on bile acid-mediated mucosal injury, diagnostic modalities (Bilitec, HIDA, endoscopy), and therapeutic options including prokinetics, sucralfate, ursodeoxycholic acid, and surgical diversion
topic.why_matters: Provides a unified clinical framework for managing bile reflux gastropathy, a condition often under-recognised in post-surgical patients and those with refractory symptoms despite acid suppression

characteristics.what_it_measures: Quality and consistency of evidence supporting UDCA in bile reflux gastropathy
characteristics.value: limited but promising; symptom and histological improvement reported in small trials
characteristics.vs_baseline: superior to no treatment in available studies; comparable to surgical diversion in mild cases
characteristics.evidence: narrative review of clinical trials and case series
characteristics.context: Post-surgical and primary bile reflux populations

good_for: clinical management of bile reflux gastropathy
requires: access to bile reflux diagnostic modalities

novelty: Consolidated clinical framework for bile reflux gastropathy diagnosis and stepwise management
Highlighted ursodeoxycholic acid as a pharmacological option for bile acid detoxification in gastropathy
open_problems: Large-scale RCTs for ursodeoxycholic acid in bile reflux gastropathy are lacking
Standardised diagnostic criteria for bile reflux gastropathy not yet established
Optimal post-surgical prophylaxis against bile reflux remains debated
